author: parithosh

author: m.kalinin
content: Will it be useful to share a small Gloas test suite with potentially imperfect coverage (payload always present and PTC vote 100% in the support of payload) to start integrating the test format extension and give the feedback to those generated tests earlier into the process?

author: potuz
content: I am not that worried by pure forkchoice tests (although those are going to be quite important) but I am worried about testing that clients can propose on top of the right head. That is, forkchoice tests are there to ensure that given a current blocktree configuration, clients agree on the right head status. There's another stage on top of agreeing on the right head status and that's being able to propose on top of that head. Every single bug I'm currently tracking on Prysm is about this latter situation more than computing the right head

author: potuz
content: The spec doesn't really have a good testsuite for this, devnets are good for this

author: m.kalinin
content:
> There's another stage on top of agreeing on the right head status and that's being able to propose on top of that head

What kind of issues are you finding with this in Gloas?

author: potuz
content: you need to pick up the right state to send payloadAttributes on FCU

author: potuz
content: and that state is tricky cause you want to have it cached

author: potuz
content: so you need to keep a cache of the post CL state advanced to the next slot in case there's no payload and of the post-payload state advanced to the next slot as soon as it arrives
